markdownRemark.html:
Introduction

  • General digital systems usually come with multiple built-in language environments, commonly including Chinese, English, Japanese, etc., and switching to different language environments displays the corresponding language.

  • In our operating system, when we need to input various form information, how can we accommodate text display in different language environments?

    • Multilingual input fields satisfy this scenario. The function of a multilingual input field is to allow input of display text in multiple language environments simultaneously.
    • It consists of a regular text input box and a suffix icon. Clicking the icon opens a popup, which contains input fields for different languages.
    • The default input box displays the text from the input field corresponding to the current language environment.
    • Note: The system language environment is managed by the LocaleProvider context in the component library.

Click to view Multilingual Component Demo and API Introduction

Next, we will introduce from two aspects: the default input box and the multilingual input box in the pop-up:

Introduction to the Default Input Box

  • Before opening the pop-up, the multilingual component is just a regular input box, with options for single-line input and multi-line input.
  • The single-line input box inherits from the TextField component, so all properties available in TextField can be set.
  • The multi-line input box inherits from the TextArea component, following the same principle.
  • The content displayed in the current input box depends on the current language setting. For example, if the current language is set to English, the default input box will display the content in English.

Settings for Multilingual Default Input Box Properties

DataSet Multilingual Field Settings

The default input box inherits from the TextField component, so all Field-related properties can be set. The following shows the settings for Default Value and Required

const ds = new DataSet({
  primaryKey: 'pk',
  // The tlsUrl attribute is the URL for multilingual query requests. When name is set, the default is /dataset/{name}/languages.
  fields: [
    {
      name: 'first-name',
      // The type of the multilingual field is intl
      type: 'intl',
      defaultValue: 'Zhangsan',
      // Required Settings
      required: true,
    },
  ],
});

Component Property Settings

  • Associate a DataSet and set the corresponding field name.
  • By setting the type property of the component, you can display it as a single-line or multi-line input. singleLine is for single-line input and is the default; multipleLine is for multi-line input.
  • Of course, you can also set properties of the TextField component, such as placeholder, clearButton, etc. For multi-line input, you can additionally set properties of the TextArea component, such as rows and resize.
<IntlField dataSet={this.ds} name="first-name" placeholder='Click the button to enter content in different languages' />

Introduction to Multilingual Input Box in Pop-up

  • Click the icon to open a pop-up, which contains input boxes for different language environments.
  • The data for the multilingual component is managed through an associated DataSet. The default language input box data is stored in the corresponding field, while the data in the other language input boxes is managed by a DataSet instantiated within the IntlField component. Each language corresponds to a field, and data queries are handled via the tlsUrl interface for fetching and submitting.
  • We can use the modalProps property of the multilingual component to set additional properties for the pop-up; in addition, the maxLengths property can be used to limit the length of input for different languages within the pop-up.

The following introduces two scenarios:

Restricting Input Length for Different Languages

maxLengths: Specifies the maximum input length for options within the multilingual pop-up (the maxLength setting for the main language field takes priority). Type: object;

The example below shows setting the maximum input length to 5 for the en_GB language input box and 10 for the en_US language input box.

<IntlField
  placeholder="Limit input length"
  dataSet={this.ds}
  name="last-name"
  maxLengths={{ en_GB: 5, en_US: 10 }}
/>

Setting required or custom validation for input fields in different languages

  • The input fields in different languages within a popup are rendered by TextField or TextArea components, depending on the type property set in the component.

  • How do we set these input fields as required or apply custom validation?

    • The data for these input fields is managed by a DataSet instance instantiated within the multilingual component, and each language input field corresponds to a different Field. The logic for the Field name is: ${tlsKey}.${name}.${key}.
    • Therefore, we can dynamically set Field-related properties for input fields in different languages.

  • When should we set these Field properties?

    • The Modal component has a hook property called beforeOpen. The beforeOpen hook is executed before the modal opens, and we can set the properties here.
import { getConfig } from 'choerodon-ui';
import { DataSet, IntlField } from 'choerodon-ui/pro';

class App extends React.Component {
  hadSetValidation = false;

  ds = new DataSet({
    primaryKey: 'pk',
    data: [{ 'first-name': '吴' }],
    tlsUrl: '/dataset/user/languages',
    fields: [
      {
        name: 'first-name',
        type: 'intl',
        defaultValue: 'Huazhen',
        required: true,
      },
    ],
  });

  beforeOpen = () => {
    if (!this.hadSetValidation) {
      const tlsKey = getConfig('tlsKey');
      const fieldName = 'first-name';
      const languageKeys = ['en_GB', 'en_US'];
      languageKeys.forEach(languageKey => {
        // Get the field for the corresponding language, e.g.: __tls.first-name.en_GB
        const field = this.ds.getField(`${tlsKey}.${fieldName}.${languageKey}`);
        if (field) {
          // Set field-related properties, Required: required
          field.set('required', true);
        }
      });
      const fieldCN = this.ds.getField(`${tlsKey}.${fieldName}.zh_CN`);
      if (fieldCN) {
        // Set field-related properties, validator: validator
        fieldCN.set('validator', (value, name, record) => {
          if (!value || value.length < 2){
            return 'Please fill in the information correctly';
          }
          return true;
        });
      }
      // Field attributes are set only once
      this.hadSetValidation = true;
    }
  }

  render() {
    return (
      <IntlField dataSet={this.ds} name="first-name" modalProps={{ beforeOpen: this.beforeOpen }} />
    );
  }
}
